highlights.summary: Patch release (0.8.1) that: (1) adds the OmnipodKit driver as a new submodule and wires it into the Add Pump flow as a unified “All Omnipod Types”/opt-in pump manager (pod type selected at activation); (2) bumps fastlane/gems in CI/release tooling to address a security dependency (jwt) resolution; and (3) fixes telemetry daily-send cadence so telemetry pings are properly throttled (~once per day).
highlights.userFacingImpact: OmnipodKit: A new "All Omnipod Types" pump manager option is available in Add Pump; users must opt in and select pod type at activation — existing OmniKit/OmniBLE setups are unchanged unless the user explicitly switches to the new driver. Telemetry: background telemetry send behavior has been changed so sends are gated and re-checked on cold launch and on each foreground transition, which should prevent continuous/more-than-daily pings. fastlane/gem bump: CI/release-tooling only and does not change shipped app code in the supplied diffs.

Fix Telemetry daily send for continous pings

items.summary: Moves the post-bolus determineBasalSync() call out of the bolus do/catch and wraps it in its own do/catch so algorithm errors during the post-bolus refresh cannot be reported as a bolus failure (see diff:5f4173f51dcd; commit:e51fc67ee0455aa6bb5120e96e053fb53bcb92c7; pr:gordolio/Trio#1151).
items.userFacingImpact: Should prevent spurious 'Bolus failed' notifications after a manual bolus when the pump has actually delivered the bolus but a subsequent algorithm refresh fails (the PR and commit cite examples such as 'Not enough glucose data' during CGM warm-up) (pr:nightscout/Trio#1151; commit:e51fc67ee0455aa6bb5120e96e053fb53bcb92c7). The change is limited to error handling around determineBasalSync(); runtime verification/testing is recommended to confirm behavior in all environments (diff:5f4173f51dcd).

sources.title: gordolio/Trio#1151: Fix spurious 'Bolus failed' notification after manual bolus
nightscout/Trio#1151: Fix spurious 'Bolus failed' notification after manual bolus
Fix spurious 'Bolus failed' notification after manual bolus

items.summary: Adds OmnipodKit as a git submodule and integrates Omnipod pump support into Trio (UI entries, OmniPumpManager, and device state handling). Also updates the OmnipodKit submodule pointer and merges upstream changes (including added translations and issue templates) (see pr:gordolio/Trio#1157; pr:gordolio/Trio#1160; diff:39e0fed83ab2; diff:ff4f3c44a8bc).
items.userFacingImpact: The app adds support files and UI entries for Omnipod-type pumps (an "All Omnipod Types" selection appears in pump selection and pump configuration) and integrates OmniPumpManager behavior into device state handling (reservoir updates, manual temp basal signaling, pod expiration and activation times). The submodule update also brings translated strings and repo templates into the referenced OmnipodKit commit. Functional verification and testing are recommended before assuming full runtime behavior for end users.

sources.title: update OmnipodKit:  translate strings, add Issue Templates

items.summary: Telemetry and build-metadata adjustments: (1) build-info capture now forces POSIX/C locale when writing com-trio-build-date to Info.plist; (2) telemetry parses build-date and emits YYYY-MM-DD (UTC) buildDate, and adds locale, timeZone, and appName fields to the payload; (3) fixes a TelemetryClient payload assignment (appName reference) to use the correct variable (TelemetryClient.telemetryAppName).
items.userFacingImpact: These changes affect telemetry and build-metadata only: build-date values written at build time are now produced in a POSIX/C locale; telemetry will report buildDate as YYYY-MM-DD (UTC) and will include locale, timeZone, and appName in its payload. The TelemetryClient variable reference fix corrects payload construction. The supplied diffs do not show changes beyond telemetry/build-metadata, but human review is recommended to confirm effects in your deployment.

items.summary: Update Config.xcconfig development version identifiers: bump APP_DEV_VERSION values for development build identifiers (no APP_VERSION change).
items.userFacingImpact: No application behavior changes are evident in the supplied diff; the edits modify only the APP_DEV_VERSION build identifier(s) in Config.xcconfig, which appear to be CI/build metadata.

includedPullRequests.title: Fix spurious 'Bolus failed' notification after manual bolus
includedPullRequests.body:
fixes #1150 
The post-bolus determineBasalSync() call was inside the bolus do/catch, so any algorithm error (e.g. 'Not enough glucose data' during CGM warm-up) was misreported as a bolus failure even though the pump had already delivered the bolus successfully. Wrap the algorithm refresh in its own do/catch so it cannot trigger bolusDidFail or a pumpError notification.

includedPullRequests.title: Fix spurious 'Bolus failed' notification after manual bolus
includedPullRequests.body:
fixes #1150 
The post-bolus determineBasalSync() call was inside the bolus do/catch, so any algorithm error (e.g. 'Not enough glucose data' during CGM warm-up) was misreported as a bolus failure even though the pump had already delivered the bolus successfully. Wrap the algorithm refresh in its own do/catch so it cannot trigger bolusDidFail or a pumpError notification.

includedPullRequests.title: Trio v0.8.1 patch release
includedPullRequests.body:
## Summary
Rolls three recently-landed tracks into a patch release: the new OmnipodKit pump manager and a security-driven dependency bump.

## What's Included

  1. OmnipodKit pump manager (Eros + DASH unified) — adds loopandlearn/OmnipodKit as a new submodule plus the wiring to surface it in the Add Pump flow as "All Omnipod Types". Pod type (Eros vs. DASH) is selected at activation rather than at the pump-manager level. OmniKit and OmniBLE remain in place unchanged — existing users on those drivers see no behavior change; the new driver is purely opt-in until a user explicitly selects it on next pod start.

  2. Security: fastlane 2.235.0 / jwt 3.2.0 — bumps fastlane 2.233.1 → 2.235.0, which relaxes its transitive jwt < 3 cap and lets the resolver land jwt 3.2.0+. Closes Dependabot alert #14 (ruby-jwt empty-key HMAC bypass, sibling of CVE-2026-44351).
  CI/release tooling only, no shipped-app code surface change. 

  3. Fix Telemetry Daily Sends — continuous background pings were not being correctly throttled to once per day. The daily send logic is now enforced properly. 

It also includes small fixes added recently to dev:
* Fix Spurious "Bolus Failed" Notification After Manual Bolus (https://github.com/nightscout/Trio/pull/1151)
* Telemetry Payload Improvements (#1172)

Pod type (Eros vs. DASH) is selected at activation rather than at the pump-manager level. includedPullRequests.title: Update telemetry payload: fix buildDate format, add locale/TZ/appName
includedPullRequests.body:
Fixes a few issues identified with the Trio telemetry payloads:

* On non-English locales, the buildDate was improperly formatted. e.g., a builder on a French language-default OS would emit `lun. mai 2`. Fix: force capture-build-details to POSIX default locale (english).
* Parse the timestamp and emit as YYYY-MM-DD instead of “Tue May 26” format
* Add iOS system locale + timezone to telemetry, with the above bug as motivation for why we should track this. Plus, this will be beneficial for users when managing translations.
* Add appName to telemetry -- hardcoded as "Trio", but can be overridden for forks

includedPullRequests.title: Update telemetry payload: fix buildDate format, add locale/TZ/appName

Fixes a few issues identified with the Trio telemetry payloads:

* On non-English locales, the buildDate was improperly formatted. e.g., a builder on a French language-default OS would emit `lun. mai 2`. Fix: force capture-build-details to POSIX default locale (english).
* Parse the timestamp and emit as YYYY-MM-DD instead of “Tue May 26” format
* Add iOS system locale + timezone to telemetry, with the above bug as motivation for why we should track this. Plus, this will be beneficial for users when managing translations.
* Add appName to telemetry -- hardcoded as "Trio", but can be overridden for forks

includedPullRequests.title: Trio v0.8.2 patch release
includedPullRequests.body:
## What's Changed At A Glance

- **OmnipodKit is now the sole Omnipod driver** — OmniBLE and OmniKit submodules removed; "All Omnipod Types" is the only Omnipod entry in Add Pump and Onboarding
- **Live Activity Enhancements / Fixes:**
   -  Temp target on chart — active temp targets now render as a green band on the Live Activity chart, with a named badge alongside the override badge
   - Ondefinite override fix — overrides with no set duration no longer collapse the chart's rule mark to the current moment
   - COB label and widget string fixes — corrected the COB string and localized all widget item labels
- **Watch connectivity fix** — massively improve watch performance; stale data on watch now updates in just a moment vs. getting stuck during processing.
- **Fix Stats Meals showing zero macros for current day** — today's carbs, fat, and protein now always appear correctly in the Meals chart
---

## Full List of PRs in `0.8.2`

| PR | Title | Author |
|---|---|---|
| [#1180](https://github.com/nightscout/Trio/pull/1180) | Add temp target display to Live Activity lock screen widget | [@t1dude](https://github.com/t1dude) |
| [#1182](https://github.com/nightscout/Trio/pull/1182) | Update CODEOWNERS: add @marionbarker and @kingst | [@dnzxy](https://github.com/dnzxy) |
| [#1184](https://github.com/nightscout/Trio/pull/1184) | Fix COB string, localize Live Activity widget item labels | [@dnzxy](https://github.com/dnzxy) |
| [#1188](https://github.com/nightscout/Trio/pull/1188) | Fix Stats Meals showing zero macros for current day | [@dnzxy](https://github.com/dnzxy) |
| [#1190](https://github.com/nightscout/Trio/pull/1190) | Fix watch app back-sync after long staleness | [@dnzxy](https://github.com/dnzxy) |
| [#1192](https://github.com/nightscout/Trio/pull/1192) | Fix indefinite override not drawn in Live Activity chart | [@t1dude](https://github.com/t1dude) |
| [#1200](https://github.com/nightscout/Trio/pull/1200) | Remove Legacy Omnipod Submodules (OmniKit, OmniBLE) | [@dnzxy](https://github.com/dnzxy) |

---

## Contributors

@dnzxy @t1dude @marv-out

includedPullRequests.title: Trio v0.8.1 patch release – sync to dev
includedPullRequests.body:
## Summary
Rolls three recently-landed tracks into a patch release: the new OmnipodKit pump manager and a security-driven dependency bump.

## What's Included

  1. OmnipodKit pump manager (Eros + DASH unified) — adds loopandlearn/OmnipodKit as a new submodule plus the wiring to surface it in the Add Pump flow as "All Omnipod Types". Pod type (Eros vs. DASH) is selected at activation rather than at the pump-manager level. OmniKit and OmniBLE remain in place unchanged — existing users on those drivers see no behavior change; the new driver is purely opt-in until a user explicitly selects it on next pod start.

  2. Security: fastlane 2.235.0 / jwt 3.2.0 — bumps fastlane 2.233.1 → 2.235.0, which relaxes its transitive jwt < 3 cap and lets the resolver land jwt 3.2.0+. Closes Dependabot alert #14 (ruby-jwt empty-key HMAC bypass, sibling of CVE-2026-44351).
  CI/release tooling only, no shipped-app code surface change. 

  3. Fix Telemetry Daily Sends — continuous background pings were not being correctly throttled to once per day. The daily send logic is now enforced properly. 

It also includes small fixes added recently to dev:
* Fix Spurious "Bolus Failed" Notification After Manual Bolus (https://github.com/nightscout/Trio/pull/1151)
* Telemetry Payload Improvements (#1172)
